compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart
compiled_state.description:
## Observed

While executing epic 6eca-183e-1cd2-4fb9 this session, `rebar review-plan <id>` began
returning INDETERMINATE / store-read-failure for a ticket whose plan had nothing wrong with
it. Root cause: `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/relation_snapshot.py`'s `tracker_head_sha()`
requires the SHARED `.tickets-tracker` worktree (checked out on the orphan `tickets` branch,
symlinked into every worktree on the machine) to have a perfectly clean `git status
--porcelain` (zero untracked/modified files) and `git ls-files -u` (no unmerged) before it
will return a usable HEAD sha for ANY ticket.

In this case, two untracked files (`*-REVIEW_RESULT.json`, `*-SIGNATURE.json`) had been left
by what was almost certainly a crashed/incomplete `rebar` process working on a COMPLETELY
UNRELATED ticket (`6673-7636-a116-4f90`). Those two orphaned files collapsed plan-review to
INDETERMINATE for every other ticket on the machine — including ones with clean, fully
plan-review-passable descriptions — with no diagnostic that pointed at the actual dirty
path(s). The only way to discover the cause was to manually `git -C .tickets-tracker status
--porcelain` and reason about it.

## Why this matters

`.tickets-tracker` is explicitly a resource SHARED across every concurrent agent session on
a machine (each epic/worktree gets a symlink to the same tracker checkout). Any agent's
crashed or slow-to-commit write anywhere in the tracker blocks `review-plan` — and therefore
`claim` — for every OTHER ticket and every OTHER agent, with a generic INDETERMINATE verdict
that gives no hint the failure is environmental rather than about the ticket's own plan.

## Suggested remediation (not investigated in depth — a starting point)

- Have `tracker_head_sha()` / the `store-read-failure` path surface the actual dirty/unmerged
  paths in its error (or the review verdict's diagnostic), not just a generic
  store-read-failure, so an agent hitting this doesn't have to reverse-engineer the cause via
  manual `git status`.
- Consider whether the clean-check truly needs to span the WHOLE tracker checkout, or could
  be scoped more narrowly (e.g. only paths touched by the review being run), to shrink the
  blast radius of one agent's crash/slow-commit onto every other concurrent reviewer.

## Non-destructive resolution used this session

Confirmed via `ps aux | grep rebar` that no process was still writing those files, confirmed
via file timestamps they were the newest artifacts in an otherwise fully-committed series for
that ticket, then committed them (matching the prior commit-message convention for that
ticket path) and pushed to `origin/tickets` — this unblocked `review-plan` for the whole
shared tracker without discarding anything.
compiled_state.title: Shared .tickets-tracker dirty state blocks review-plan for EVERY ticket, not just the affected one
